channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: The banality of Jeffery Epstein’s expanding online world
item.description: In recent weeks, a project called Jmail.world has quickly recreated the online life of Jeffrey Epstein, the late financier and convicted sex offender with myriad ties to the rich and powerful.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Heilweil, Fast Company

item.title: How to watch Inoue vs Picasso: live stream boxing online, start time, ringwalks, fight card, PPV cost
item.description: All the ways to watch Inoue vs Picasso live streams online, including PPV options as the Monster defends his undisputed super bantamweight crown against El Rey.
item.dc:creator: Andy Murray, TechRadar

item.title: From OpenAI to Nvidia, here’s a list of recent multibillion-dollar AI deals
item.description: Nvidia has agreed to license technology from AI startup Groq for use in some of its artificial intelligence chips, marking the chipmaker’s largest deal and underscoring its push to strengthen competitiveness amid surging demand.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, Fast Company

item.title: The next cyber battlefield: Preparing federal networks for autonomous malware
item.description: The mission for federal defenders is clear: build systems and cultures resilient enough to meet an adversary that learns as fast as it attacks. The post The next cyber battlefield: Preparing federal networks for autonomous malware first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network

item.title: High severity flaw in MongoDB could allow memory leakage
item.description: Document database vendor MongoDB has advised customers to update immediately following the discovery of a flaw that could allow unauthenticated users to read uninitialized heap memory.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Acquisition more than IT drove the news in 2025
item.description: Federal IT and acquisition experts say the FedRAMP update and artificial intelligence pilots moving to production were among the big story lines last year. The post Acquisition more than IT drove the news in 2025 first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network

item.title: Most parked domains now push scams and malware
item.description: Cybersecurity firm Infoblox reveals that over 90 percent of parked domains now redirect visitors to scams and malware, making simple typos extremely dangerous.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Nvidia reaches technology licensing deal with startup Groq
item.description: Nvidia Corp. agreed to a licensing deal with artificial intelligence startup Groq, furthering its investments in companies connected to the AI boom and gaining the right to add a new type of technology to its products.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg, The Mercury News

item.title: Manchester United vs Newcastle live streams: How to watch Premier League 2025-26 from anywhere in the world, team news
item.description: All the ways to watch Manchester United vs Newcastle live streams online and from anywhere in the only Premier League fixture on Boxing Day.
item.dc:creator: Adrian Back, TechRadar

item.title: ServiceNow $12 billion deal spree is ‘deja vu’ of CEO’s SAP days
item.description: After years of eschewing big mergers, ServiceNow Inc. is on a deal spree. It has spent at least $12 billion this year on acquisitions or strategic investments.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg, The Mercury News

item.title: The Switch 2 has had a brilliant first year, but cracks in performance are already starting to show
item.description: Nintendo’s year was dominated by Switch 2 speculation and the arrival of the system, but there’s plenty more that happened, too.
item.dc:creator: Lloyd Coombes, TechRadar

item.title: Digitalizar y cooperar para… saber y poder cuidar o curar
item.description: La transformación digital que se está produciendo en el sector salud no sigue un esquema planificado, ni responde a una estrategia explícita planteada por alguno de los múltiples actores que interactúan en nuestro sistema sanitario.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: This year I'm buying Blu-rays again like it's 2016 – here's why you should too
item.description: I'll be spending less on streaming in 2026 and buying Blu-ray instead. It isn't just about nostalgia either –here's why it makes sense.
item.dc:creator: Carrie Marshall, TechRadar

item.title: Gmail may finally be giving users the chance to change their primary email address
item.description: It seems Google is rolling out the ability to set a new primary @gmail.com account, while keeping your old one.
item.dc:creator: David Nield, TechRadar

item.title: When scaling AI means foundational reform
item.description: AI pilot projects are often executed quickly and successfully. But the real challenge is scaling the solution across the entire enterprise, says Mauro Macchi, Accenture’s EMEA CEO.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Reader picks: The most popular Python stories of 2025
item.description: Python 3.14 was the star of the show in 2025, bringing official support for free-threaded builds, a new all-in-one installation manager for Windows, and subtler perks like the new template strings feature.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Google’s new AI tool Disco can turn tabs into apps
item.description: Google is launching Disco, a new experiment for the Chrome browser built on Gemini 3. The tool allows users to turn open tabs into interactive web apps, known as “GenTabs”, which can be used for study or travel planning, for example.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Pro-Russian hackers claim cyberattack on French postal service
item.description: French prosecutors say a pro-Russian hacking group has claimed responsibility for a major cyberattack on France’s national postal service.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Get started with Python’s new native JIT
item.description: JITing, or “just-in-time” compilation, can make relatively slow interpreted languages much faster. Until recently, JITting was available for Python only in the form of specialized third-party libraries, like Numba, or alternate versions of the Python interpreter, like PyPy.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: The 5 big events that shaped Microsoft’s 2025
item.description: This has been a pivotal 12 months for Microsoft, a year in which it faced increasing competition that threatened to knock the company off its perch as the world’s leader in AI.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Rust vision group seeks enumeration of language design goals
item.description: To help the Rust language continue scaling across domains and usage levels, the Rust Vision Doc group recommends enumerating the design goals for evolving the language while also improving the crates package system.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: A new honor for a leader who’s shaped cybersecurity policy and talent across sectors
item.description: "I do believe that it's not just a mission, it's a calling. And I would encourage every young person to take advantage of the opportunity," said Diana Burley The post A new honor for a leader who’s shaped cybersecurity policy and talent across sectors first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Terry Gerton, Federal News Network

item.title: US cyber progress isn’t stalled
item.description: Beneath the policy layer, the technical and strategic modernization of U.S. cybersecurity is actually accelerating faster than ever. The post US cyber progress isn’t stalled — it’s evolving first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Sean Davis, Federal News Network

item.title: Data center investments break all records
item.description: New data centers are being built around the world in connection with AI initiatives. According to a compilation by S&P Global, spending will reach a record $61 billion in 2025, with the US and Canada accounting for the vast majority.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Google sues data scraping company
item.description: Google is now suing US data scraping company Serpapi for using hundreds of millions of fake search queries to bypass Google’s protection system and illegally obtain copyrighted material from search results, Reuters reports.
item.dc:creator: Computer World
